title: neottia bracteosa
intro: Neottia bracteosa, also known as the grass-pink orchid, is a rare and endangered epiphytic orchid species native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Neottia bracteosa include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as the promotion of wound healing and immune system function. Neottia bracteosa is often prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although its availability and usage should be carefully considered due to its endangered status.
